name: Markdown Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Markdown Workflow. This workflow integrates 12 different services: stickyNote, markdown, httpRequest, chainLlm, outputParserStructured. It contains 22 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
### Get your lemlist API key

1. Go to your lemlist account or create one [HERE]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

2. Go to Settings -> Integrations

3. Generate your API Key and copy it

4. On this node, click on create new credential and paste your API key

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
# Read me

This workflow send email replies of your lemlist campaigns to the Slack channel of your choice.

The OpenAI node will classify the reply status. 

The Slack alert is structured in a way that make it easy to read for the user.

nodes.name: Categorize lemlist reply

parameters.text:
=Classify the [email_content] in one only of the following categories: 

Categories=["Interested", "Out of office", "Unsubscribe", "Not interested", "Other"] 

- Interested is when the reply is positive, and the person want more information or a meeting 

Don't output quotes like in the next example: 
email_content_example:Hey I would like to know more 
category:Interested

email_content:"{{ $json.textClean }}" 

Only answer with JSON in the following format:
{"replyStatus":category}

JSON:

=Classify the [email_content] in one only of the following categories: 

Categories=["Interested", "Out of office", "Unsubscribe", "Not interested", "Other"] 

- Interested is when the reply is positive, and the person want more information or a meeting

nodes.name: Route reply to the right branch

parameters.content:
### Get your lemlist API key

1. Go to your lemlist account or create one [HERE]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

2. Go to Settings -> Integrations

3. Generate your API Key and copy it

4. On this node, click on create new credential and paste your API key

# Read me

This workflow send email replies of your lemlist campaigns to the Slack channel of your choice.

The OpenAI node will classify the reply status. 

The Slack alert is structured in a way that make it easy to read for the user.

### Create a Slack notification for each new replies

1. Connect your Slack account by clicking to add Credentials

2. Write the name of the channel where you want to send the Slack alert
### Save time by automatically unsubscribing leads that don't want to receive emails from you
### Mark interested leads as interested in lemlist
### This node will clean the text and make sure it looks pretty on Slack
### Trigger a different scenario according to the category of the reply

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Lemlist Trigger - On new reply".
Open the "Format text with Markdown" node (n8n-nodes-base.markdown) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send alert to Slack" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Lemlist - Unsubscribe" node (n8n-nodes-base.lemlist) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "lemlist - Mark as interested" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Categorize lemlist reply" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge data" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Route reply to the right branch" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
